Gene Set Module Scoring

Source: vignettes/module-scoring.Rmd
module-scoring.Rmd

Overview

ComputeModuleScore() computes per-cell gene set activity scores using three methods: AUCell, Seurat AddModuleScore, and UCell. It is an S3 generic that works with both raw matrices and Seurat objects.

Supported Input Formats

Gene sets can be provided in three formats:

1. Named List 

gene_sets <- list(
  "Hallmark_TNFA"   = c("JUNB", "CXCL2", "ATF3", "NFKBIA", "TNFAIP3"),
  "Hallmark_HYPOXIA" = c("VEGFA", "SLC2A1", "ALDOA", "ENO1", "LDHA")
)

2. Data Frame (term-gene pairs) 

gene_sets_df <- data.frame(
  term = c(rep("TNFA_SIGNALING", 5), rep("HYPOXIA", 5)),
  gene = c("JUNB", "CXCL2", "ATF3", "NFKBIA", "TNFAIP3",
           "VEGFA", "SLC2A1", "ALDOA", "ENO1", "LDHA")
)

3. GMT File Path 

gmt_path <- system.file("extdata/gmt/h.all.v2022.1.Hs.symbols.gmt",
                         package = "scMMR")

Scoring Methods

AUCell (Default)

Area Under the recovery Curve – ranks genes by expression and computes the AUC of gene set members in the top-ranked genes:

scores <- ComputeModuleScore(
  object    = expression_matrix,  # genes x cells
  gene.sets = gene_sets,
  method    = "AUCell"
)
# Returns: pathways x cells matrix

Seurat AddModuleScore

Computes the average expression of gene set genes minus the average of control gene sets:

scores <- ComputeModuleScore(
  object    = expression_matrix,
  gene.sets = gene_sets,
  method    = "Seurat",
  nbin      = 24,
  ctrl      = 100
)

UCell

Mann-Whitney U statistic-based scoring, robust to library size differences:

scores <- ComputeModuleScore(
  object    = expression_matrix,
  gene.sets = gene_sets,
  method    = "UCell"
)

Using with Seurat Objects

The Seurat method wraps the default and stores results directly:

library(Seurat)

# Store as a new assay (recommended for multi-pathway scoring)
seu <- ComputeModuleScore(
  object    = seu,
  gene.sets = gmt_path,
  method    = "AUCell",
  store     = "assay",
  assay.name = "Hallmark"
)

# Access scores
seu[["Hallmark"]]@data[1:3, 1:5]

# Store as metadata columns (simpler for a few pathways)
seu <- ComputeModuleScore(
  object    = seu,
  gene.sets = gene_sets,
  method    = "Seurat",
  store     = "metadata",
  prefix    = "Score_"
)

head(seu@meta.data[, grep("Score_", colnames(seu@meta.data))])

Working with GMT Files

Read and Parse GMT 

# Read GMT file
gs <- read_gmt(system.file("extdata/gmt/h.all.v2022.1.Hs.symbols.gmt",
                            package = "scMMR"))
length(gs)  # 50 Hallmark gene sets
names(gs)[1:5]

# Flexible parsing (auto-detects format)
gs2 <- parse_gene_sets(gmt_path)                  # GMT file
gs3 <- parse_gene_sets(gene_sets)                  # Named list (pass-through)
gs4 <- parse_gene_sets(gene_sets_df)               # Data frame

Filter by Gene Set Size 

# Keep only gene sets with 15-500 genes
gs_filtered <- gs[sapply(gs, length) >= 15 & sapply(gs, length) <= 500]

Bundled GMT Databases

scMMR ships with 14 GMT files covering major pathway databases:

Database Species File Gene Sets
MSigDB Hallmark Human h.all.v2022.1.Hs.symbols.gmt 50
KEGG Legacy Human c2.cp.kegg.v2022.1.Hs.symbols.gmt 186
Reactome Human reactome.gmt 1,000+
GO Biological Process Human GO_bp.gmt 7,000+
CollecTRI (regulons) Human collectri.human.gmt 700+
PROGENy (signaling) Human progeny.human.top100.gmt 14
Proliferation Human proliferation.combined.gmt Custom
Reactome Mouse m_reactome.gmt 1,000+
GO Biological Process Mouse m_GO_bp.gmt 7,000+

Comparison of Methods

Feature AUCell Seurat UCell
Score range [0, 1] Unbounded [0, 1]
Speed Moderate Fast Moderate
Library size robust Yes Partial Yes
Best for Regulatory activity Quick exploration Small gene sets

Tips

  • Use method = "AUCell" for gene regulatory network scoring
  • Use method = "Seurat" for quick exploratory analysis
  • Use method = "UCell" when comparing across batches with different depths
  • Filter gene sets to 15-500 genes for meaningful scores
  • Use store = "assay" when scoring many pathways to keep metadata clean

On this page
